methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Ouros Energy is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Ethan Loosbrock that has raised $3M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints to support a reliable peer-median valuation—only zero seed-stage and zero near-stage comparables met the filtering threshold, making a sector-wide median approach uninformative.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here. Using the $3M total raised as a base value proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $7.5M. This method captures the expected appreciation in company value as Ouros moves toward Series A, though it does not incorporate sector-specific benchmarking or operating metrics beyond funding history.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, with a median of $8M. The range reflects conservative (15% below median) and optimistic (15% above median) scenarios around the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable seed-stage transactions in the narrow Climate & Energy vertical, limiting calibration to stage-based progression mechanics alone. Any valuation refinement would require additional operating data, market traction metrics, or a deeper peer universe at equivalent financing stages.
